Title: Dev mode and editing help entries
Summary: How to enable Help authoring. Why it is gated by an env var.
Body:
## The flag

Set `BLEEPFORGE_DEV_MODE=1` in the environment before starting the server. Either in your shell:

```
export BLEEPFORGE_DEV_MODE=1
pnpm dev
```

Or in a project local `.env`. Restart the server after the flag changes; it is captured at boot, not hot swapped.

## What it unlocks

When the flag is set:

- The Help list page shows a "+ New category" button.
- Each category section shows "edit category" and "+ entry" actions.
- Each entry view shows an "edit" button that opens the inline editor.
- Server side, PUT and DELETE on `/api/help/...` are accepted.

When the flag is unset:

- All of the above are hidden client side.
- Server side, PUT and DELETE on `/api/help/...` return 403 even if a stale browser tab tries to send them. Defense in depth.

## Why an env var, not a Preference

Help authoring is a build time concern. End users of a packaged Bleepforge build should not be able to edit help entries through a UI toggle. The env var keeps the surface gated by who can launch the server, which is the right granularity.

## How the entries are stored

Help content lives in JSON under `data/help/<categoryId>/`. Each category folder has `_meta.json` for the category schema and one `<entryId>.json` per entry. The format is documented in [the body markup entry](/help/under-the-hood/help-body-markup).
